commentary: Eleven sessions this week versus twelve last week, so volume is basically even — but the shape of the week is noticeably different, and not entirely in a bad way. The running dropped right off, down from two runs totalling 20km to just the one half-marathon on Saturday, which is worth keeping an eye on, though your swim distances held solid with three sessions at 2000m or better and your heart rate on those swims is creeping down compared to equivalent efforts last week, which means you are genuinely getting fitter in the water whether you feel it or not. The B&B swim with the little pink hearts in the name absolutely got noticed, and the fact that you followed it with an afternoon weights session the same day suggests the legs and the ego are both in good shape. The walk on Thursday is either a very mature recovery choice or evidence that something was tired, and either way it counts, but the real story this week is four weights sessions to last week's five — close enough — and a body that is quietly, consistently doing the work even when the week gets messy.
